articles.adaptedFrom: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism – Digital News Report 2026
articles.title: More People Around the World Are Avoiding the News, Major Report Finds
articles.summary: A large study on how people read and watch news around the world has found that many people are feeling worried and overwhelmed by current events. The report found that around 42 out of every 100 people sometimes choose to avoid the news on purpose. Researchers say this is partly because of worries about false information spreading online. The study also found that more and more people are getting their news from social media and AI tools instead of traditional newspapers or TV, which experts say can make it harder to tell what is true. The report covered readers across dozens of countries in 2026.

lv1: What did the report find that many people are doing with the news? (這份報告發現很多人對新聞有什麼反應？)
From where are more and more people getting their news, according to the study? (根據這份研究，越來越多人從哪裡獲取新聞？)
lv2: Why do you think some people might want to avoid watching or reading the news? (你認為為什麼有些人可能想要避開新聞？)
Why might getting news from social media or AI tools be less trustworthy than traditional news sources? (為什麼從社交媒體或人工智能工具獲取新聞，可能比傳統新聞來源更不可靠？)
lv3: If nearly half of all people avoid the news, what problems could this cause for society and democracy? Do you think news organisations should change how they report to win back audiences? (如果近一半的人刻意迴避新聞，這對社會和民主制度可能帶來什麼問題？你認為新聞機構應否改變報道方式以重新吸引讀者？)

Some argue that AI-generated news summaries make it easier to stay informed, while others worry they spread misinformation. Which side do you agree with more, and why? (有人認為人工智能生成的新聞摘要讓人更容易了解時事，但也有人擔心這會散播錯誤資訊。你更同意哪一方，為什麼？)

articles.adaptedFrom: BBC News / 明報即時新聞
articles.title: Scorching Heat Above 40°C Hits Southern Spain as Summer Heatwave Grips Europe
articles.summary: Southern Spain has been experiencing extremely hot weather, with temperatures climbing above 40 degrees Celsius in the Andalusia region. The intense heat has forced both people and animals to look for ways to cool down — dogs were seen eating watermelon and playing in pools and on beaches to escape the blazing sun. Scientists have long warned that climate change is making heatwaves hotter, longer and more common across Europe and other parts of the world. Authorities have urged residents and visitors to stay hydrated, avoid going outside during the hottest parts of the day, and check on elderly neighbours who may be especially at risk during such extreme conditions.

lv1: How hot did it get in southern Spain during the heatwave? (西班牙南部的熱浪期間，氣溫升至多少度？)
What were some animals doing to try to keep cool? (一些動物做了什麼來降溫？)
lv2: Why are elderly people considered to be especially at risk during a heatwave? (為什麼長者在熱浪期間被認為風險特別高？)
How is climate change connected to the increasing number of extreme heatwaves we are seeing around the world? (氣候變化與全球越來越多極端熱浪有什麼關係？)
lv3: Some governments tell people to change their daily habits to fight climate change, such as using less electricity or driving less. Do you think individual actions are enough to solve the problem, or does real change have to come from governments and big companies? (有些政府要求市民改變日常習慣來應對氣候變化，例如減少用電或少開車。你認為個人行動足以解決問題嗎，還是真正的改變必須來自政府和大企業？)

As heatwaves become more frequent, some cities are redesigning themselves with more trees, parks and cool zones. Should Hong Kong invest more in such changes? What challenges might it face? (隨著熱浪越來越頻繁，一些城市正透過種植更多樹木、公園和清涼區來重新規劃城市。香港是否應投入更多資源進行這類改變？可能面對什麼挑戰？)

articles.title: 香港擬立法規管網約車　最快今年第四季開放申請牌照
articles.summary: 香港政府計劃立法規範網約車服務，例如Uber等手機應用程式叫車平台。根據初步方案，當局預計最快於今年第四季開始接受網約車平台及司機的牌照申請，並計劃於同年十一月起正式向平台發出牌照。此舉旨在確保乘客安全，並對司機的駕駛資格設立統一要求。市民對新規管制度反應不一，有人認為有助提升服務質素，亦有人關注對現有的士業界造成衝擊。

lv1: 政府計劃幾時開放網約車牌照申請？
立法規管網約車的主要目的是什麼？
lv2: 為什麼政府需要為網約車服務制定專門的法例，而不是沿用現有的士法規？
網約車規管對乘客、司機和傳統的士業界各有什麼影響？
lv3: 有意見認為，規管網約車可以保障乘客安全；但也有人擔心過多規管會限制科技創新和市場競爭。你認為政府應如何在兩者之間取得平衡？
香港的士業界長期面臨網約車的競爭壓力。政府在推動創新科技的同時，應如何照顧傳統行業從業員的利益？

articles.title: 美國氣象局警告：史上最強厄爾尼諾或於2026年底形成
articles.summary: 美國國家海洋及大氣管理局（NOAA）發出警告，指一股可能是有史以來最強烈的厄爾尼諾現象，正在逐步形成，預計在二○二六年十一月至二○二七年一月之間達到頂峰。厄爾尼諾是一種影響全球天氣的自然現象，會令部分地區出現嚴重旱災，另一些地方則面臨洪水和暴雨。科學家預測，屆時全球各地發生極端天氣事件的機會將大幅增加，呼籲各國政府盡早做好防災準備，保護市民安全。

lv1: 發出厄爾尼諾警告的是哪個機構？
厄爾尼諾現象預計在哪段時間達到最強？
lv2: 厄爾尼諾現象會對不同地區的天氣造成什麼不同影響？
為什麼科學家要提早發出預警，而不等到現象真正出現才通知公眾？
lv3: 厄爾尼諾等極端天氣現象與人類活動引起的氣候變化之間有什麼關係？我們是否有能力減少這類自然現象的發生頻率？
面對可能出現的史上最強厄爾尼諾，香港和亞洲各地政府應採取哪些具體措施，才能有效保護市民？你認為個人又可以做些什麼？
